2603.19849.title: Semantic Delta: An Interpretable Signal Differentiating Human and LLMs Dialogue
2603.19849.paper_summary: Do LLMs talk like us? This question intrigues a multitude of scholar and it is relevant in many fields, from education to academia. This work presents an interpretable statistical feature for distinguishing human written and LLMs generated dialogue. We introduce a lightweight metric derived from semantic categories distribution. Using the Empath lexical analysis framework, each text is mapped to a set of thematic intensity scores. We define semantic delta as the difference between the two most dominant category intensities within a dialogue, hypothesizing that LLM outputs exhibit stronger thematic concentration than human discourse. To evaluate this hypothesis, conversational data were generated from multiple LLM configurations and compared against heterogeneous human corpora, including scripted dialogue, literary works, and online discussions. A Welch t-test was applied to the resulting distributions of semantic delta values. Results show that AI-generated texts consistently produce higher deltas than human texts, indicating a more rigid topics structure, whereas human dialogue displays a broader and more balanced semantic spread. Rather than replacing existing detection techniques, the proposed zero-shot metric provides a computationally inexpensive complementary signal that can be integrated into ensemble detection systems. These finding also contribute to the broader empirical understanding of LLM behavioural mimicry and suggest that thematic distribution constitutes a quantifiable dimension along which current models fall short of human conversational dynamics.
2603.19849.paper_summary_zh: LLM 是否像我們一樣交談？這個問題引起了眾多學者的興趣，並且在許多領域中都具有相關性，從教育到學術界。這項工作提出了一種可解釋的統計特徵，用於區分人類撰寫的對話和 LLM 生成的對話。 我們引入了一種輕量級的度量，源自語義類別的分佈。使用 Empath 詞彙分析框架，每段文本都被映射到一組主題強度分數。 我們定義語義差異為對話中兩個最主導類別強度之間的差異，假設 LLM 輸出表現出比人類話語更強的主題集中度。 為了評估這一假設，從多個 LLM 配置生成了對話數據，並與異質的人類語料庫進行比較，包括劇本對話、文學作品和在線討論。 對語義差異值的結果分佈應用了 Welch t 檢驗。 結果顯示，AI 生成的文本始終產生比人類文本更高的差異，這表明主題結構更為僵化，而人類對話則顯示出更廣泛和更平衡的語義分佈。 提出的零樣本度量並不是取代現有的檢測技術，而是提供了一種計算上便宜的補充信號，可以整合到集成檢測系統中。 這些發現還有助於對 LLM 行為模仿的更廣泛實證理解，並表明主題分佈構成了一個可量化的維度，而當前模型在此方面未能達到人類對話的動態。
2603.19849.author: Riccardo Scantamburlo et.al.
2603.19849.authors: Riccardo Scantamburlo, Mauro Mezzanzana, Giacomo Buonanno, Francesco Bertolotti
